questions.q: For which cellular structure did Venkatraman Ramakrishnan share the Nobel Prize?
questions.explanation: Ramakrishnan was honoured for revealing the atomic structure of the ribosome, the cell's protein-making machine.

questions.q: In which year did Venkatraman Ramakrishnan win his Nobel Prize?
questions.explanation: He was awarded the Nobel Prize in 2009 for his work on the ribosome.

questions.q: In which category was Ramakrishnan's Nobel Prize awarded?
questions.explanation: The 2009 prize for ribosome structure was the Nobel Prize in Chemistry.

questions.q: In which country was Venkatraman Ramakrishnan born?
questions.explanation: He was born in 1952 in Chidambaram, Tamil Nadu, India.

questions.q: What is the main job of the ribosome that Ramakrishnan studied?
questions.explanation: The ribosome translates messenger RNA into proteins, a process called translation.

questions.q: Ramakrishnan's subtitle in this deck calls him the 'Mapper of the' what?
questions.explanation: He is celebrated as the structural biologist who mapped the atomic structure of the ribosome.

questions.q: The ribosome reads instructions carried by which molecule to build proteins?
questions.explanation: Ribosomes translate the genetic code carried by messenger RNA (mRNA) into chains of amino acids.

questions.q: Proteins are built from chains of which building blocks, assembled by the ribosome?
questions.explanation: Ribosomes link amino acids together in the order specified by mRNA to form proteins.

questions.q: Ramakrishnan later took on a prominent leadership role at which scientific body?
questions.explanation: He served as President of the Royal Society, the UK's national academy of sciences, from 2015 to 2020.

questions.q: Why are ribosomes important targets for understanding antibiotics?
options: Many antibiotics work by attacking bacterial ribosomes
questions.explanation: Many antibiotics kill bacteria by binding their ribosomes and blocking protein synthesis, so the structures help drug design.

questions.q: With whom did Ramakrishnan share the 2009 Nobel Prize in Chemistry?
questions.explanation: He shared the prize with Thomas A. Steitz and Ada E. Yonath for studies of the structure and function of the ribosome.

questions.q: Which experimental technique was central to determining the ribosome's atomic structure?
questions.explanation: Ramakrishnan and colleagues used X-ray crystallography to map the positions of atoms in the ribosome.

questions.q: Ramakrishnan's prize-winning ribosome work focused mainly on which subunit?
questions.explanation: He is especially known for determining the atomic structure of the small 30S ribosomal subunit.

questions.q: At which UK institution did Ramakrishnan carry out his Nobel-winning ribosome research?
options: The MRC Laboratory of Molecular Biology in Cambridge
questions.explanation: He performed the work at the Medical Research Council Laboratory of Molecular Biology (LMB) in Cambridge, England.

questions.q: In which Indian town was Venkatraman Ramakrishnan born in 1952?
questions.explanation: He was born in Chidambaram in Tamil Nadu in 1952.

questions.q: In what subject did Ramakrishnan earn his first university degree before turning to biology?
questions.explanation: He earned a bachelor's degree in physics in India and a PhD in physics in the US before moving into biology.

questions.q: The ribosome that Ramakrishnan studied is made of proteins and which other type of molecule?
questions.explanation: Ribosomes are ribonucleoprotein complexes built from ribosomal RNA (rRNA) and many proteins.

questions.q: Which honour did Ramakrishnan receive from the British monarch in 2012?
questions.explanation: He was knighted in 2012, becoming Sir Venkatraman Ramakrishnan.

questions.q: Determining the ribosome structure has most directly aided the development of which kind of medicine?
questions.explanation: Because many antibiotics target the ribosome, the atomic maps guide the design of new antibiotic drugs.

questions.q: Ramakrishnan holds citizenship of which country in addition to his Indian birth?
questions.explanation: He became a US citizen during his long scientific career in the United States before moving to the UK.

questions.q: Ramakrishnan wrote a candid memoir about the competitive ribosome research effort. What is it called?
questions.explanation: His 2018 memoir 'Gene Machine' recounts the race to solve the ribosome's structure.

questions.q: The process by which the ribosome converts the genetic code into a protein is called what?
questions.explanation: Translation is the ribosome-mediated process of reading mRNA codons to assemble a protein.

questions.q: Which Israeli scientist who shared the 2009 prize is credited with pioneering ribosome crystallisation decades earlier?
questions.explanation: Ada Yonath pioneered the crystallisation of ribosomes in the 1980s, paving the way for the structural work.

questions.q: Ramakrishnan's structures helped explain how the ribosome ensures accurate translation by monitoring base pairing. This proofreading concept is known as decoding what?
questions.explanation: His 30S subunit structures revealed how the ribosome checks correct codon-anticodon pairing to ensure translational fidelity.

questions.q: Before settling at the LMB in Cambridge, Ramakrishnan did key ribosome work at which US laboratory in the 1990s?
questions.explanation: He worked at Brookhaven National Laboratory and later the University of Utah before moving to Cambridge in 1999.

questions.q: A central long-standing question the ribosome structures helped answer was whether the catalytic core is made of protein or RNA. What did the structures support?
options: The ribosome is a ribozyme (RNA does the catalysis)
Protein alone forms the active site
DNA folds to catalyse the reaction
questions.explanation: The structures supported that the ribosome is a ribozyme, with ribosomal RNA forming the peptidyl transferase catalytic centre.

questions.q: Ramakrishnan earned his PhD in physics in 1976 from which US university?
questions.explanation: He completed his PhD in physics at Ohio University before retraining in biology.

questions.q: Which term refers to the heavy-atom and phasing problem that ribosome crystallographers had to overcome to interpret their diffraction data?
questions.explanation: Solving the crystallographic 'phase problem' for such a huge complex was a major hurdle in determining the ribosome structure.

questions.q: Ramakrishnan's small-subunit structures clarified how certain antibiotics like aminoglycosides cause errors. They act by binding which part of the ribosome?
options: The decoding site of the 30S subunit
questions.explanation: Aminoglycoside antibiotics bind the decoding region of the small (30S) subunit, disrupting translational accuracy, as his structures illuminated.

questions.q: Ramakrishnan was awarded the 2010 Padma Vibhushan. What is this honour?
options: One of India's highest civilian awards
questions.explanation: The Padma Vibhushan, which he received in 2010, is among India's highest civilian honours.
